title: Iran 'floating armoury' seizure — May 14 2026
narrative: BBC (May 14, 2026) — Iran's Revolutionary Guard seized a vessel described as a "floating armoury" in the Gulf of Oman. Official Iranian state media called it a "Coastal patrol operation." Western officials, speaking anonymously, called it a "provocation." No ship name, flag, or operator has been officially confirmed. The UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) agency issued a warning but no details.
telemetry: AIS data for the Gulf of Oman region should show vessel movements for May 13-14 — was the ship broadcasting, or was AIS intentionally darkened?
Satellite imagery providers (Planet, Sentinel-2, ICEYE) may have captured the vessel at anchor or in port — check for before/after positioning
USNI, DriveOnGulf, and naval blogs (e.g., "The Drive" / WarZone) may have ship ID, photos, or crew manifests
Iranian state media (IRNA, PressTV) coverage — does the official narrative match the "floating armoury" framing?
Regional shipping insurance / Lloyds market bulletins may identify the vessel and its cargo
The phrase "floating armoury" itself is a media construction — verify whether any military equipment was actually documented
verdict: [SIPHONED VERDICT] Official narrative is incomplete. AIS data, satellite imagery, and operational details contradict or remain unconfirmed.
